message:
perf(mvcc): ActiveEdgeDiscoveryIndex uses PageNumberBuildHasher + pre-sized cap

The four PageNumber-keyed HashMaps in `ActiveEdgeDiscoveryIndex` were
using the default `RandomState` hasher and grew from 0 capacity via the
hashbrown doubling chain (0 → 3 → 7 → 15 → 31 → ...). The MT 16t
profile sampled `RawTable<(PageNumber, Vec<usize>)>::reserve_rehash` at
1.03% self-time under `plan_concurrent_commit → ActiveEdgeDiscoveryIndex::build`
(see tests/artifacts/perf/bd-m4s2c-20260423T013614Z/perf-report-mt_writers_16x500.txt).

Switch to `PageMap<Vec<usize>>` (the existing
`HashMap<PageNumber, V, PageNumberBuildHasher>` alias) for identity
hashing on the u32 page number, and pre-allocate `views.len() * 4`
capacity in `build()` so the rehash chain is skipped for realistic
MT workloads.

The two `HashMap<(PageNumber, u64), Vec<usize>>` tuple-keyed maps are
left on `RandomState` — they did not appear in the profile and switching
would require a new hasher for the tuple key.

Before: median 43,522 ns/build (mean 43,598, variance ±0.8%, n=7)
After:  median 30,363 ns/build (mean 30,377, variance ±0.7%, n=7)
Delta:  -30.2% on the index build path
Bench:  bench_active_edge_index_build (added here; 16 views × 10
        pages/view matching the MT 16t profile scenario)
Cmd:    rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc4_mvcc \
        cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        bench_active_edge_index_build -- --ignored --nocapture

Correctness: all 71 begin_concurrent::tests pass, including the
ssi_validation edge-discovery tests (test_discover_incoming_from_hot_plane,
test_discover_incoming_from_recently_committed,
test_discover_outgoing_from_commit_index,
test_discover_outgoing_from_hot_plane).

Bead: bd-oy6qr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
